---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Welche Arten von Daten gibt es?

<style>
.dt-grid {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 0.8em;
  margin-top: 0.3em;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.dt-col {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  position: relative;
}
.dt-hdr {
  color: white;
  font-family: sans-serif;
  font-size: 12px;
  font-weight: bold;
  padding: 6px 12px;
  border-radius: 5px 5px 0 0;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.dt-body {
  border-top: none;
  border-radius: 0 0 5px 5px;
  padding: 10px 12px;
  font-size: 0.79em;
  flex: 1;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
.dt-chip {
  background: #f0f0f0;
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 3px 7px;
  font-size: 0.88em;
  display: inline-block;
  margin: 2px 2px;
}
.dt-hint {
  padding: 6px 9px;
  font-size: 0.84em;
  line-height: 1.5;
  border-radius: 0 3px 3px 0;
  margin-top: 8px;
}
.dt-synth {
  margin-top: 0.5em;
  border: 1px solid #b3b2b2;
  border-radius: 5px;
  padding: 6px 14px;
  background: #fafafa;
  font-size: 0.79em;
  line-height: 1.6;
}
/* ── ER-Badges: untere rechte Ecke, minimal überlappend ── */
.er-badge {
  position: absolute;
  color: white;
  font-family: sans-serif;
  font-size: 11px;
  font-weight: bold;
  padding: 6px 12px;
  border-radius: 5px;
  border: 2px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.55);
  box-shadow: 0 3px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.28);
  transform: rotate(40deg);
  opacity: 0;
  z-index: 10;
}
.er-018 {
  background: rgba(80, 36, 121, 0.90);
  bottom: 49px;
  right: -13px;
  animation: popIn 0.45s ease-out 1.0s forwards;
}
.er-016 {
  background: rgba(213, 0, 6, 0.88);
  bottom: 5px;
  right: 9px;
  animation: popIn 0.45s ease-out 1.7s forwards;
}
@keyframes popIn {
  from { opacity: 0; transform: rotate(40deg) scale(0.65); }
  to   { opacity: 1; transform: rotate(40deg) scale(1); }
}
</style>

<div class="dt-grid">

<!-- ── Strukturiert ── -->
<div class="dt-col">
  <div class="dt-hdr" style="background:#7d0a52;">
    📊 Strukturierte Daten
  </div>
  <div class="dt-body" style="border:2px solid #7d0a52;">
    <strong>Tabellarisch · festes Schema · direkt analysierbar</strong>
    <div style="margin:8px 0;">
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-excel', fill='#217346')` Excel / CSV</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('database', fill='#336791')` SQL</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('table', fill='#686868')` ERP</span>
    </div>
    <table style="font-size:0.9em; border-collapse:collapse; width:100%; margin:4px 0 8px 0;">
      <tr style="background:#f9f0f3;">
        <th style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:left; border:1px solid #e0c8d0;">Filiale</th>
        <th style="padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #e0c8d0;">Umsatz</th>
        <th style="padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #e0c8d0;">Preis</th>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">Hamburg</td>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:center; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">12.4</td>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:center; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">89</td>
      </tr>
      <tr style="background:#fdf6f8;">
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">Berlin</td>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:center; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">8.1</td>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:center; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">95</td>
      </tr>
    </table>
    <div class="dt-hint" style="background:#f9f0f3; border-left:3px solid #7d0a52;">
      `r fa('circle-right')` Taddy (2019): <strong>„flat file"</strong><br>
      1 Zeile = 1 Beobachtung<br>
      1 Spalte = 1 Merkmal<br>
      → direkt für ML nutzbar
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ── Unstrukturiert + ER-Badges ── -->
<div class="dt-col">
  <div class="dt-hdr" style="background:#D50006;">
    🎵 Unstrukturierte Daten
  </div>
  <div class="dt-body" style="border:2px solid #D50006;">
    <strong>Kein Schema · ~80% aller Unternehmensdaten</strong>
    <div style="margin:8px 0;">
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-word', fill='#2b579a')` Word</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-pdf', fill='#e74c3c')` PDF</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-lines', fill='#555')` E-Mail / Text</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('music', fill='#e74c3c')` MP3</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('video', fill='#8e44ad')` MP4</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('image', fill='#16a085')` JPG / PNG</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('comment', fill='#686868')` Social Media</span>
    </div>
    <div class="dt-hint" style="background:#fff0f0; border-left:3px solid #D50006;">
      `r fa('circle-right')` Lange schwer nutzbar —<br>
      erst <strong>Foundation Models</strong> (GPT etc.)<br>
      erschließen diesen Typ<br>
      in großem Maßstab
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="er-badge er-018">ER018</div>
  <div class="er-badge er-016">ER016</div>
</div>

<!-- ── Semi-strukturiert ── -->
<div class="dt-col">
  <div class="dt-hdr" style="background:#502479;">
    🔗 Semi-strukturierte Daten
  </div>
  <div class="dt-body" style="border:2px solid #502479;">
    <strong>Flexibles Schema · verschachtelt (nested)</strong>
    <div style="margin:8px 0;">
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('code', fill='#e67e22')` JSON</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-code', fill='#2980b9')` XML</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('globe', fill='#27ae60')` HTML</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('envelope', fill='#686868')` E-Mail-Header</span>
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:0.82em; background:#f4f4f4; border-radius:4px;
                padding:5px 8px; margin:6px 0; font-family:monospace;
                line-height:1.4; color:#333;">
      {"filiale": "Hamburg",<br>
      &nbsp;"umsatz": 12.4,<br>
      &nbsp;"prod": [{"id":1,"p":89}]}
    </div>
    <div class="dt-hint" style="background:#f5f0fa; border-left:3px solid #502479;">
      `r fa('circle-right')` Taddy: <strong>„nested data"</strong><br>
      Rel. DB: normalisierte Tabellen<br>
      Für ML: erst <strong>flatten</strong>!<br>
      (denormalisieren)
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

</div><!-- dt-grid -->

<div class="dt-synth">
`r fa('circle-right')` <strong>Taddy (2019):</strong>
ML-Algorithmen erwarten fast immer ein <strong>flat file</strong> —
unstrukturierte und nested Daten müssen erst aufbereitet werden.
&nbsp;&nbsp;`r fa('circle-right')` <strong>Feature Engineering</strong> = dieser Aufbereitungsschritt (→ Lab 2)
&nbsp;&nbsp;`r fa('circle-right')` <strong>Carseats:</strong> bereits flat — 400 × 11, direkt nutzbar.
</div>

???
* nested → flat: oft teuerster Schritt im Data Engineering
* Normalisierung (3NF): verhindert Redundanz, erzeugt aber Joins
* flatten = denormalisieren = Feature Matrix für ML
* ER018 / ER016: Module die sich mit unstrukturierten Daten beschäftigen
